treatment_information.treatment_response: Improvement in potassium levels; however, patient initially felt nauseous and lightheaded.

treatment_timeline.event: Patient admitted for nausea, vomiting, and hypotension. Initial labwork showed hypokalemia and hyponatremia.
Patient received IV fluids and potassium supplementation.
Home medications including amiodarone and atorvastatin were held.
Sodium levels improved back to normal.
Patient discharged with instructions not to restart held medications until follow-up.

patient_outcome.follow_up_details: Patient advised to attend all follow-up appointments and resume medications only after consulting a primary care physician.
patient_outcome.long_term_prognosis: Expected to continue improving; no restrictions on diet or fluid intake.
patient_outcome.patient_perspective: Patient was informed about the causes of her symptoms and the plan for follow-up.
